    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/accessors.go", "symbol": "WebhookAccessor"},

  "chain": [
    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go", "symbol": "chainAdmissionHandler"},
    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go", "symbol": "Plugin"},
    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go", "symbol": "mutatingDispatcher"},
    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/plugin.go", "symbol": "Plugin"},
    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/dispatcher.go", "symbol": "validatingDispatcher"},
    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/webhook.go", "symbol": "Webhook"}
  ],
  "text": "The Kubernetes API server admission webhook chain implementation spans multiple packages within the apiserver codebase:\n\n## Core Webhook Controller Architecture\nThe foundation is the generic webhook controller (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/webhook.go`), which implements the `Webhook` type - an abstract admission plugin with infrastructure for both Admit and Validate operations. The `NewWebhook()` function creates instances accepting handler, configFile, sourceFactory, and dispatcherFactory parameters. The `Dispatch()` method serves as the entry point for webhook dispatch logic, orchestrating calls to the dispatcher with collected hooks from the source.\n\n## Webhook Dispatcher Implementations\nTwo dispatcher implementations handle the actual webhook invocation pattern:\n\n1. **Mutating Webhooks** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go`): The `mutatingDispatcher` type implements the Dispatcher interface, iterating through webhooks and applying patches. The `callAttrMutatingHook()` method handles individual webhook calls, creates admission objects, verifies responses, and applies JSON patches to the object. Crucially, it manages webhook re-invocation context (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/reinvocationcontext.go`), tracking which webhooks should be re-invoked if mutations occur.\n\n2. **Validating Webhooks** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/dispatcher.go`): The `validatingDispatcher` implements parallel webhook invocation using goroutines and WaitGroups. The `callHook()` method calls individual validation webhooks, and errors are collected and returned. This dispatcher caches versioned attributes to avoid redundant conversions across parallel webhook calls.\n\n## Plugin Registration\nThe mutating and validating plugins (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go` and `validating/plugin.go`) wrap the generic Webhook, exposing `Admit()` and `Validate()` methods that delegate to the underlying `Webhook.Dispatch()` call.\n\n## Admission Request/Response Handling\nThe request package (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go`) manages AdmissionReview object creation and response verification. `CreateAdmissionObjects()` constructs v1 or v1beta1 AdmissionReview requests, while `VerifyAdmissionResponse()` validates responses and extracts audit annotations, patches, and warnings. `CreateV1AdmissionReview()` and `CreateV1beta1AdmissionReview()` handle version-specific object construction.\n\n## Webhook Accessors and Configuration\nWebhook accessors (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/accessors.go`) provide abstraction over mutating and validating webhook configurations, enabling both v1 and v1beta1 access patterns. The initializer package (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/initializer/initializer.go`) handles plugin initialization with service resolvers and authentication info.\n\n## Admission Chain Integration\nThe core chain handler (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go`) orchestrates the admission control chain, executing handlers in sequence for mutating and validating phases. It implements `chainAdmissionHandler` type that iterates through registered admission handlers, separating MutationInterface (Admit) and ValidationInterface (Validate) handlers.\n\nThis architecture provides a complete webhook chain with:\n- Dynamic webhook configuration management via informers\n- Sequential mutating and parallel validating webhook dispatch\n- Request/response normalization across multiple AdmissionReview versions\n- Patch application and object mutation tracking\n- Webhook re-invocation for dependent mutations\n- Comprehensive error handling with fail-open/fail-closed policies\n- Audit annotation and warning propagation"
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Kubernetes API Server Admission Webhook Chain

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement the API server admission webhook chain: the webhook admission controller, the webhook call dispatch, and the admission response aggregation.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the domain domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all the Go source files implementing the API server admission webhook chain in kubernetes/kubernetes. Let me start by exploring the available resources and searching for the relevant code.
Let me search more systematically for the webhook implementation files:
Now let me examine the core webhook implementation files to understand the complete chain:

Based on my comprehensive search of the kubernetes/kubernetes repository, I've identified all the Go source files that implement the API server admission webhook chain. Let me create the answer file with the complete findings.

Perfect! I've successfully identified and documented all the Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement the API server admission webhook chain. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Components Identified:

### **Core Architecture Files (11 files)**
1. **Generic Webhook Controller** - `generic/webhook.go` - The base admission webhook with dispatcher integration
2. **Webhook Dispatcher Interface** - `generic/interfaces.go` - Defines Dispatcher, Source, and WebhookInvocation interfaces
3. **Mutating Webhook Plugin** - `mutating/plugin.go` - Plugin wrapper for mutating webhooks
4. **Mutating Dispatcher** - `mutating/dispatcher.go` - Implements sequential webhook invocation with patch application
5. **Validating Webhook Plugin** - `validating/plugin.go` - Plugin wrapper for validating webhooks
6. **Validating Dispatcher** - `validating/dispatcher.go` - Implements parallel webhook invocation
7. **AdmissionReview Handler** - `request/admissionreview.go` - Creates and verifies admission requests/responses
8. **Webhook Accessors** - `webhook/accessors.go` - Abstracts webhook configuration access
9. **Plugin Initializer** - `initializer/initializer.go` - Handles plugin setup
10. **Reinvocation Context** - `mutating/reinvocationcontext.go` - Tracks webhook re-invocation state
11. **Admission Chain Handler** - `chain.go` - Orchestrates the complete admission handler chain

### **Key Functionality Implemented:**
- **Webhook Dispatch**: Both sequential (mutating) and parallel (validating) dispatch patterns
- **Request/Response Handling**: Full AdmissionReview object creation and response verification
- **Patch Application**: JSON patch application for mutating webhooks
- **Webhook Re-invocation**: Context management for re-invoking webhooks when mutations occur
- **Error Handling**: Fail-open/fail-closed policies based on webhook failure policy
- **Audit Annotations**: Support for audit annotations and warnings in webhook responses

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with detailed file paths, symbol definitions, and a comprehensive narrative explaining the webhook chain architecture.

  "text": "The Kubernetes API server admission webhook chain implementation spans multiple packages within the apiserver codebase:\n\n## Core Webhook Controller Architecture\nThe foundation is the generic webhook controller (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/webhook.go`), which implements the `Webhook` type - an abstract admission plugin with infrastructure for both Admit and Validate operations. The `NewWebhook()` function creates instances accepting handler, configFile, sourceFactory, and dispatcherFactory parameters. The `Dispatch()` method serves as the entry point for webhook dispatch logic, orchestrating calls to the dispatcher with collected hooks from the source.\n\n## Webhook Dispatcher Implementations\nTwo dispatcher implementations handle the actual webhook invocation pattern:\n\n1. **Mutating Webhooks** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go`): The `mutatingDispatcher` type implements the Dispatcher interface, iterating through webhooks and applying patches. The `callAttrMutatingHook()` method handles individual webhook calls, creates admission objects, verifies responses, and applies JSON patches to the object. Crucially, it manages webhook re-invocation context (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/reinvocationcontext.go`), tracking which webhooks should be re-invoked if mutations occur.\n\n2. **Validating Webhooks** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/dispatcher.go`): The `validatingDispatcher` implements parallel webhook invocation using goroutines and WaitGroups. The `callHook()` method calls individual validation webhooks, and errors are collected and returned. This dispatcher caches versioned attributes to avoid redundant conversions across parallel webhook calls.\n\n## Plugin Registration\nThe mutating and validating plugins (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go` and `validating/plugin.go`) wrap the generic Webhook, exposing `Admit()` and `Validate()` methods that delegate to the underlying `Webhook.Dispatch()` call.\n\n## Admission Request/Response Handling\nThe request package (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go`) manages AdmissionReview object creation and response verification. `CreateAdmissionObjects()` constructs v1 or v1beta1 AdmissionReview requests, while `VerifyAdmissionResponse()` validates responses and extracts audit annotations, patches, and warnings. `CreateV1AdmissionReview()` and `CreateV1beta1AdmissionReview()` handle version-specific object construction.\n\n## Webhook Accessors and Configuration\nWebhook accessors (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/accessors.go`) provide abstraction over mutating and validating webhook configurations, enabling both v1 and v1beta1 access patterns. The initializer package (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/initializer/initializer.go`) handles plugin initialization with service resolvers and authentication info.\n\n## Admission Chain Integration\nThe core chain handler (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go`) orchestrates the admission control chain, executing handlers in sequence for mutating and validating phases. It implements `chainAdmissionHandler` type that iterates through registered admission handlers, separating MutationInterface (Admit) and ValidationInterface (Validate) handlers.\n\nThis architecture provides a complete webhook chain with:\n- Dynamic webhook configuration management via informers\n- Sequential mutating and parallel validating webhook dispatch\n- Request/response normalization across multiple AdmissionReview versions\n- Patch application and object mutation tracking\n- Webhook re-invocation for dependent mutations\n- Comprehensive error handling with fail-open/fail-closed policies\n- Audit annotation and warning propagation"
}
